{"idea": {"Name": "spectral_precision_routing", "Title": "Spectral Precision Routing: Training-Free Dynamic Bit-Width Allocation via Activation Geometry Diagnostics", "Short Hypothesis": "The hypothesis posits that layer-wise activation covariance condition numbers and SVD decay rates directly bound numerical error amplification under reduced precision. This direction is necessary because static mixed-precision and calibration-based PTQ fail under dynamic distribution shifts, while heuristic sensitivity metrics lack formal guarantees. Investigating this via training-free geometric diagnostics is optimal because it bypasses costly retraining or calibration datasets, directly leveraging intrinsic activation geometry as a zero-overhead proxy for numerical stability.
